direction: Ancient Empires-inspired turn-based tactics with village capture, commander-led armies, a large frontier campaign, and classic unit-counter warfare on mobile.
camera_perspective: top-down orthographic square-grid battlefield with light tactical elevation cues
control_model: tap-select unit control with contextual move attack capture recruit wait and commander-order actions, drag camera panning, and dedicated end-turn plus threat-range toggles
core_loop_signature: capture villages and keeps for gold, recruit and rotate counter units across large authored maps, leverage terrain and commander orders, and defeat regional warlords to reclaim the frontier chapter by chapter
differentiation_axes: large authored square-grid campaign maps instead of wave lanes or small skirmish rooms
village income and recruit-structure tempo rather than passive build-slot defense economy
commander-led war roster with terrain warfare and counter matchups instead of physics puzzle or action timing
multi-front battle objectives including escort holdout fortress assault and breakthrough chapters
persistent marshal doctrine and hero continuity across a six-region campaign
forbidden_template_reuse: no one-screen skirmish loop reused as the whole campaign
no auto-battler resolution replacing direct unit control
no tiny 8 x 8 production map budget
no generic fantasy reskin with copied economy roster and mission structure from another project

map_content_budget.play_area_model: six-region campaign made of 30 authored square-grid battle maps with multiple fronts, capturable structures, and chapter-specific objectives
map_content_budget.route_or_region_count: 6 macro regions and 30 maps minimum, with most maps offering 2 to 4 approach lines or pressure points
map_content_budget.decorative_prop_count: at least 24 recurring prop families across banners, barricades, braziers, stones, carts, statues, tents, ruins, siege debris, trees, fence lines, and dock clutter
map_content_budget.camera_or_scroll_model: drag-pannable battlefield camera with optional zoom, tile-locked selection, and quick recenter on active unit or commander

special_rules: terrain defense and movement costs materially alter matchup value
captured villages and keeps change income and forward recruitment tempo
commander orders create limited burst turns without replacing core positioning play
combat should expose a readable forecast before commitment
chapter objectives must vary and not default to rout-only wins
enemy armies are AI-controlled by default and must show distinct easy normal and hard behavior presets instead of relying only on raw stat cheating
risk_reward_systems: fast village capture accelerates income but overextends fragile units
side objectives grant stronger campaign rewards at the cost of tempo
preserving named heroes unlocks later regional advantages
aggressive early pushes can shorten maps but expose commanders to counterattacks
failure_pressure_variants: full army collapse with no recruit structure remaining

secondary_game_art_packs: campaign map and landmark prop pack
asset_variety_budget.animation_tier: medium-high for commanders cavalry flyers and elites, medium for core infantry ranged units healers and siege pieces
asset_variety_budget.minimum_distinct_sprite_families: minimum 18 major sprite families across allied units enemy units commanders structures terrain landmarks projectiles and capture banners
asset_variety_budget.asset_reuse_note: do not collapse the game into one generic troop sheet, one repeated village tile, and one fortress background; the asset plan must support faction identity, terrain variety, and chapter-scale readability
